Chișinău apartment report
Listings (left) + Leaflet map (right). Click a listing to zoom/highlight the marker; click a marker to jump to the listing.
What you’re seeing: apartments for purchase (source-specific). Sector borders are approximate OSM admin relations. Late-evening busyness is a composite proxy within ~300m: food/drink density + transit density + culture venues + plaza/pedestrian-zone signal + pedestrian-friendly street length.
(filters)
Overlays (100m grid): each square shows OSM POI counts binned into a 100m×100m grid over Chișinău; color intensity is log-scaled density within that grid cell.
Map legend: pin confirmed pin unconfirmed pin mismatch
#1
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€169,950 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Apartament de vânzare – 3 camere, 96,6 mp – Chișinău, Ciocana, str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2”.

Additional stated detail: “Suprafață totală: 96,6 mp”. Also noted: “2 băi dotate cu podea caldă”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €169 950. (Structured field)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Locație excelentă: în raza a 5 minute de mers pe jos se află grădiniță, școală, piață, supermarketuri (inclusiv Kaufland), bancă”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~400m of the pin shows: 8 grocery shops, 15 food/drink places, 6 parks/playgrounds, and 9 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~400m of the pin): food/drink density ~29.8/km², grocery density ~15.9/km², transit-stop density ~17.9/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

  • Price: €169,950
  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Ciocana

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~400m of the pin shows: 4 grocery shops, 4 food/drink places, 15 parks/playgrounds, and 10 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

(Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 8 grocery shops, 12 food/drink places, 2 parks/playgrounds, and 7 transit stops. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 6 grocery shops, 10 food/drink places, 53 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~28.3/km², grocery density ~28.3/km², transit-stop density ~17.7/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

  • Price: €199,900
  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Ciocana

(Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Продаётся 3-х комнатная квартира площадью 80 кв.м., с автономным отоплением. Комнаты раздельные, кухня 9 кв.м. с выходом на лоджию, длинный 6 м. балкон на обе спальни.Дом котельцовый (чешка), этаж 3 из 5, середина.”.

The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €95 000. (Structured field)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 5 grocery shops, 4 food/drink places, 2 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Street (from listing fields): șos. Hâncești.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~130.9/km², grocery density ~24.8/km², transit-stop density ~21.2/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 3 food/drink places, 148 parks/playgrounds, and 4 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În apropiere se află: Bancă, Farmacie, Grădiniță, Piață, Școală, Supermarket!”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 7 food/drink places, 4 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

(Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 1 grocery shops, 0 food/drink places, 9 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În imediata apropiere se află magazinele: Linella, Andy's Pizza, sala de fitness Premier fitness.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 5 food/drink places, 94 parks/playgrounds, and 9 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 11 food/drink places, 101 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Fit (who this suits)

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În imediată apropiere se găsesc numeroase puncte sociale și comerciale, supermarket, farmacie, bancă, grădiniță, școala, sală de fitness, etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 10 grocery shops, 5 food/drink places, 16 parks/playgrounds, and 11 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Bloc nou, Amplasare reușită aproape de marketuri, farmacii, școli și transport public.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 6 grocery shops, 10 food/drink places, 53 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).
